summary: Iteration 28 - Tested validation feedback and signature placement below stamp. VERIFIED: (1) Signature area with 'Sign Here' text appears BELOW stamp when Certification type is selected. (2) Backend correctly places signature below stamp in PDF. (3) Validation is implemented via BUTTON DISABLED state rather than toast error - button is disabled when document or recipient name is missing.

design_issues.issue: Validation feedback: User requested toast error listing missing fields when Generate is clicked, but current implementation disables button until all fields are filled. Toast validation code exists but never triggers because button is disabled first.
design_issues.current_behavior: Button disabled when fileData missing OR localRecipientName empty (line 2241)
design_issues.requested_behavior: Show toast with list of missing fields when clicking Generate without required data

backend_api_tests: POST /api/auth/login - Login with test credentials - PASS
POST /api/documents/stamp (certification with signature) - Creates stamp with signature below - PASS
POST /api/documents/stamp (certification with placeholder) - Creates stamp with 'Sign Here' placeholder - PASS
POST /api/documents/stamp (notarization) - Creates stamp without signature area - PASS
Backend signature placement code verified - signature_y = pdf_y - signature_height - 5 - PASS
frontend_ui_tests: Certification type button clickable and selectable - PASS
Signature preview 'Sign Here' appears below stamp when Certification selected - PASS
Signature area has dashed border with brand color - PASS
Rnd container height includes signatureAreaHeight (150px stamp + 30px signature = 180px) - PASS
Generate button correctly disabled when recipient name empty - PASS
Generate button enabled when all required fields filled - PASS
Signature Option section shows when Certification selected - PASS

signature_preview_below_stamp.description: When Certification stamp type is selected, a dashed border box with 'Sign Here' text appears BELOW the stamp in the document preview
signature_preview_below_stamp.observed_behavior: signatureAreaHeight=30 adds extra height to Rnd container, dashed border div rendered below stamp preview
signature_preview_below_stamp.screenshot_evidence: type_02_stamp_preview.png shows 'Sign Here' box below circle stamp

backend_signature_placement.description: Backend places signature BELOW stamp (not inside) in generated PDF

validation_behavior.user_request: Clicking Generate without Document should show toast error listing missing fields
validation_behavior.actual_implementation: Button is DISABLED when document or recipient name is missing - prevents clicking
validation_behavior.toast_code_exists: Yes, lines 681-716 have toast validation but never triggers because button is disabled first

action_items: OPTIONAL: If user wants toast validation instead of disabled button, modify line 2241 to remove disabled condition and let handleStampDocument's toast validation handle errors
critical_code_review_comments: Template stamp_type 'official' doesn't match STAMP_TYPES which only has 'certification' and 'notarization' - when template is applied, it sets selectedType to 'official' which doesn't show signature area until user manually clicks Certification
seed_data_creation: Test stamps created during backend testing with prefixes TEST_Validation*, TEST_SignaturePlaceholder, TEST_NotarizationNoSignature
context_for_next_testing_agent: Signature preview BELOW stamp VERIFIED: When Certification stamp type is selected (manually click Certification button), a 'Sign Here' dashed box appears below the stamp preview. The signature area height (30px) is added to the Rnd container (150+30=180px total). Backend places signature below stamp at signature_y = pdf_y - signature_height - 5. NOTE: Templates have stamp_type 'official' which doesn't match frontend STAMP_TYPES ('certification'/'notarization'), so user must manually select Certification to see signature area. Validation is done via DISABLED button state rather than toast - button disabled when !fileData or !localRecipientName.
